message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Implementing click.edit() multi-file support: edit_file -> edit_files, filename param accepts str or iterable

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor for CPU priority control of editor subprocess

type: INTENT
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Renaming Editor.edit_file(filename) to Editor.edit_files(filenames) accepting str|Iterable[str]; termui.edit() filename param becomes str|Iterable[str]|None. Will keep your process_priority param signature intact if present when I edit - please rebase edit_file calls to edit_files after I push, or tell me your exact signature so I can merge it in.

type: ANSWER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: My exact signature: Editor.__init__ adds process_priority: str|None=None (stored as self.process_priority); edit_file(self, filename) computes Popen kwargs (creationflags on WIN via _WIN_PRIORITY_CLASSES dict, preexec_fn calling os.nice() on Unix via _UNIX_NICE_LEVELS dict) based on self.process_priority, then passes **popen_kwargs to subprocess.Popen; termui.edit() adds process_priority: str|None=None param passed through to Editor(...). When you rename edit_file->edit_files, please keep the priority-kwargs-computation block and apply it inside edit_files per-file Popen call.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done: edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) makes ONE subprocess.Popen call joining all quoted filenames into a single command string (e.g. editor "a" "b"), not per-file - please add your process_priority kwargs computation to that single Popen call in edit_files. termui.edit() now builds a filenames tuple from str|Iterable[str] and calls ed.edit_files(filenames). Feel free to layer process_priority param on top of Editor.__init__ and edit()/edit_files() - no structural conflict expected.
